steps.title: Read the probability evidence
steps.content: The passage compares swap-line probabilities: 0.20 for countries open to foreign-capital inflows and 0.03 for countries with restrictive policies. That gap shows the policy environment was meaningfully relevant to swap-line decisions.

steps.title: Avoid overstating the link
steps.content: Choice B would mean decisions were based entirely on policy environment, which the probabilities do not show. Choice C says the decisions were unrelated to policy, the opposite of the data. Choice D implies an intermediary not mentioned. The remaining phrase means significant to. The answer is A.
